model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current run appears optimization-limited rather than unstable; increasing early channel width is a simple capacity boost that often improves CIFAR-10 feature quality within short fixed-step budgets without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With width already improved, adding one extra pooling block should increase feature hierarchy while reducing classifier input dimensionality, often yielding better validation loss on CIFAR-10 at similar parameter scale under short training budgets.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With deeper/wider features now in place, decoupled weight decay can improve optimization-generalization balance under the same 256-step budget with a minimal change to training dynamics.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The AdamW switch slightly regressed validation loss; reverting to Adam restores the previously better optimizer dynamics while keeping the stronger depth/width and schedule changes intact.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing depth and width, a modestly higher OneCycle peak LR may improve early progress within 256 steps while staying close to the current stable regime.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With stronger convolutional features now learned, the current small MLP head may bottleneck class separation; increasing hidden width is a targeted capacity increase with low implementation complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size exposes the model to more unique training examples within the same 256 updates, which can improve validation loss when optimization is already stable.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After the strong batch-size increase, the effective gradient noise is lower; raising peak LR can better utilize OneCycle’s exploration phase and improve progress within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last major gain came from larger batches under fixed-step evaluation; increasing to 256 further expands data coverage per optimizer step and is a direct continuation of the strongest observed trend.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Given the strong gains from larger batch size, a further LR increase is a low-complexity way to better match optimization scale and potentially improve convergence within the fixed 256-step horizon.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        pct_start=0.2,","new":"        pct_start=0.1,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With high batch and high max LR, shortening the warmup portion of OneCycle should allocate more of the fixed 256 steps to high-learning and decay phases, which can improve final validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        pct_start=0.1,","new":"        pct_start=0.2,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The reduced warmup fraction regressed loss, so reverting to the prior OneCycle phase split restores the better-performing schedule while preserving all other strong settings.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps and a high-batch stable regime, slightly reducing weight decay can lower underfitting pressure and improve fit without changing optimization mechanics.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest improvements have come from increasing examples processed per fixed optimizer step; moving from 256 to 384 continues that trend while being less aggressive than a direct jump to 512.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Performance has improved consistently with larger batches under fixed-step evaluation; increasing to 512 is the most direct high-upside continuation of the dominant successful pattern.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After scaling batch size to 512, a proportional LR increase is a low-complexity way to maintain effective update magnitude and may unlock additional fixed-step convergence.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"As training becomes more optimization-efficient with large batch and higher LR, regularization may now be slightly too strong for a 256-step horizon; a small weight-decay reduction can improve fit with minimal disruption.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At batch size 512 with reduced weight decay, a final moderate LR increase can improve fixed-step progress if the run is still slightly under-updating per step.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.8e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Learning-rate increases have repeatedly yielded the largest gains in the current high-batch regime, so a final moderate step up is the highest-upside single change for the last iteration.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}
